boundary_reason: A broad anti-complexity concept card claims exceptional value and near-proof confidence together while remaining proposal-level and largely pattern-alignment based.
classification_rationale: Both exceptional-value pressure and proposal-confidence pressure point toward overvaluation faster than the available evidence can justify.
why_not_likely_overvaluation: Not applicable here because this case is meant to anchor the clear overvaluation side of the boundary.
why_not_justified_exception: The card stays too broad, too future-facing and too weakly grounded to read as a bounded exception.
why_not_adjacent_classification: This does carry meaningful signal, but the combination of proposal status, cross-domain ambition and exceptional scoring is too strong to treat as a justified exception. It is not merely borderline because both value and confidence lean toward exceptional claims without exceptional proof density.
bounded_reading: Read as an important proposal that should stay high-signal without being encoded as a settled registry-scale exception.

source_snapshot.overvaluation_signal: exceptional value and proposal confidence are both elevated on a broad concept card without dense proof
source_snapshot.defensibility_signal: the concept does have real architectural resonance and non-trivial signal
source_snapshot.review_anchor: Soft-flag review identifies broad future-facing concept cards and proposed high-confidence cards as the clearest inflation zones.
